briefing: Revolutionize your search experience with Google's new desktop app for Windows.

articles.title: CodeQL 2.25.2 adds Kotlin 2.3.20 support and other updates
articles.snippet:
CodeQL is the static analysis engine behind GitHub code scanning, which finds and remediates security issues in your code. We’ve recently released CodeQL 2.25.2, which brings a new Kotlin version…
The post CodeQL 2.25.2 adds Kotlin 2.3.20 support and other updates appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
articles.ai_summary: CodeQL 2.25.2 has been released, adding support for Kotlin 2.3.20 and other updates to enhance GitHub's static analysis capabilities for security issues. This update is significant as it improves developers' ability to identify vulnerabilities in their code.
articles.why_it_matters: Enhances security analysis tools for developers using GitHub.

articles.title: I tried Google's new desktop app for Windows, and I'll never search the old way again
articles.snippet: Now available to all, the app delivers a faster way to access tools like Gemini, Lens, and Search. See why it's totally worth a download.
articles.ai_summary: Google's new desktop app for Windows offers a faster way to access tools like Gemini, Lens, and Search, promising a more efficient user experience. This release could change how users interact with Google's services on their desktops.
articles.why_it_matters: Revolutionizes desktop search and tool access for users.

articles.title: Anthropic's Project Glasswing CVE tally is still anyone's guess
articles.snippet: Like the majority of the companies participating, it remains a mystery Last week, Anthropic surprised the world by declaring that its latest model, Mythos, is so good at finding vulns that it would create chaos if released. Now, under the title of Project Glasswing, over 50 selected companies and orgs are allowed to test the hyped up LLM to find security holes in their own products. But just how many problems have they really discovered?…
articles.ai_summary: Anthropic's Project Glasswing allows select companies to test its new model, Mythos, for finding vulnerabilities, but the actual number of discovered issues remains unclear. This initiative raises questions about the model's safety and effectiveness.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights the challenges in deploying advanced AI for security testing.

articles.title: Don't let the bot play doctor! AI gets early diagnoses wrong 80% of the time
articles.snippet: 'LLMs should not be trusted for patient-facing diagnostic reasoning,' boffins advise People ask AI for all kinds of advice, including the kind of questions you'd ask a physician. However, the next time you're tempted to query ChatGPT if that growth on your face is skin cancer, consider this: research shows today's leading AI models fail at early differential diagnosis in more than 8 out of 10 cases.…
articles.ai_summary: Research indicates that AI models, including LLMs, are inaccurate in early diagnostic reasoning, getting it wrong 80% of the time. This raises concerns about the reliability of AI in medical contexts.
articles.why_it_matters: Underscores the limitations of AI in critical healthcare applications.

articles.title: Customers revolt as GitHub Copilot 'fixes' rate limits
articles.snippet: Repair of bug that undercounted token usage leads to rapid exhaustion of subscription allowance Microsoft's GitHub last week told Copilot customers that they'd have to reduce their use of the AI service to ease the strain on company servers. This follows the company's discovery last month of a token counting bug that appears to have broken the company's pricing model.…
articles.ai_summary: GitHub Copilot users are facing issues due to a bug that miscalculated token usage, leading to rapid exhaustion of their subscription allowances. This has sparked customer dissatisfaction and concerns about the service's reliability.
articles.why_it_matters: Reveals potential flaws in AI service pricing models and user trust.
